title: Versioning, Delete Markers, Object Lock, and Restore · Diagnostic Repair Bay
summary: A Node incident packet contains a plausible immutable-retention and recovery runbook with one unsafe assertion, stale object identity, unbounded stream, lost rejection, abort leak, cache defect, policy gap, or recovery overclaim; preserve the symptom and isolate first cause. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in immutable-retention and recovery runbook and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-ca…
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, distinguish unversioned, enabled, and suspended versioning and test null versions, overwrite behavior, and lifecycle impact.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, list versions and delete markers, resolve current reads, restore by removing a marker or copying a version, and preserve audit identity.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, apply governance, compliance, legal hold, retention date, and authorized bypass by version with irreversible-risk review.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Versioning State Transition
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 8231 during a Node stream backpressure incident. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to narrow versioning state and require version-specific retention and restore evidence; separate deterministic browser evidence from controlled Node and AWS transfer gates; then replace one typed fixture with unknown runtime input and prove rejection before AWS use. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, distinguish unversioned, enabled, and suspended versioning and test null versions, overwrite behavior, and lifecycle impact.

Inspect the Versioning State Transition trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Versioning State Transition.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for in a strict node 24 and typescript 7 service, distinguish unversioned, enabled, and suspended versioning and test null versions, overwrite behavior, and lifecycle impact. For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Versioning State Transition, keep Versioning State Transition connected to executable TypeScript storage and delivery decision evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Restore Drill Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: storage-ts-restore-drill-evidence-v9dm4j" that uses case 8613 to narrow versioning state and require version-specific retention and restore evidence. Incident diagnosis: preserve the symptom, isolate first cause, and prove a regression case before repair. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node or AWS SDK packages, open sockets, contact S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, networks, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with the TypeScript 7.0.2 native compiler and the @typescript/typescript6 package 6.0.2 compiler that reports 6.0.3, Node 24.18.0, AWS SDK v3.1087.0, controlled AWS, stream and handle, load, fault, security, cost, restore, and production gates.

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Restore Drill Evidence.
